module_mood_contract: earned trust, before the earning — student leaves the lecture with the stance 'these frameworks are real, the authoring move is real, the team kit is real'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers; vocab-introduction lectures land mid-module.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The lecture sits in earn-the-trust.md between open-the-side-quest (setup) and Ex1/Ex2/Ex3 (content). Body is dominantly vocabulary-and-lineage priming (lines 18: Kohnfelder/Garg 1999, Shostack 2014, Saltzer & Schroeder 1975; line 26: Klaassen + Intercom 267-skill / 31% R&D), which the rule explicitly places mid-module where the vocab earns itself. One slice — 'Why this proportion matters' (lines 28-30) — reads as pure meta-frame and would land harder as a Debrief beat. Filed as TODO under rule 47 escape-hatch list rather than blocking, because (a) the section is one short paragraph in an otherwise opener-shaped lecture, (b) the engineer audience reads the claim cleanly at opener position without it stealing the felt-evidence (the proportion is a stance the student holds going in, not a recognition they need to feel emerge), (c) cutting it would weaken the trust contract the lecture sets up.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: MAINTAINER-ACCEPTED 2026-05-15 (b) keep in lecture: engineer audience parses proportion-as-stance cleanly at opener position; moving to Debrief would lose the upfront 'deliberate ratio, not constraint' framing.

maintainer_acceptance.reason: Engineer audience holds proportion-as-stance at opener; cutting weakens the trust contract.

rules_evaluated.evidence: M3 mood contract is 'earned trust', not contrast-mood (which is M5's rescue). Rule does not fire on M3.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named; mood-floor 8+/10 on teaching beats.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Trace phase mood 8, close mood 8. Maintainer block line 63 names the target ('earned trust, before the earning. Students leave the lecture with the stance "these frameworks are real, the authoring move is real, the team kit is real" — not "we're about to do security homework."'). Lecture body honors: STRIDE / Saltzer & Schroeder lineage paragraph (line 18) does the trust work because the frameworks have a paper trail; Intercom 267-skill / 31% R&D anchor (line 26) makes team-kit accretion real rather than aspirational.
No shame or have-done-wrong framing anywhere. Lecture leads with positive disciplines — earn, invoke, author, ship.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Title 'Skills from the frontier, skills of your own' and module-level 'Earn the trust' both lead with positive disciplines. Failure-mode references inside the lecture (line 49: 'Don't make general what you don't practice yourself') frame the discipline, not a punitive corrective.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity check — don't front-run downstream teaching moments.
rules_evaluated.evidence: M4 (memory + first experiment) and M5 (rescue / packaging) teaching moments stay untouched. No bare module-number references in lecture body. M2 callback (line 12: 'second-pass walk-down you ran in Module 2') is a recognition beat, not a front-run; it identifies a primitive M3 will now name (skill = the move M2 inlined as a plain prompt) — strategy doc § M2-detail line 302 explicitly designs this reveal at M3.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room outside text.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No authored 'share with your neighbor', 'pause and discuss', 'take 90 seconds' cues. Lecture body is about the work (what the three exercises do, what artifacts they produce, why curation tracks what they do).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lecture file has no fenced prompts. Forcing functions for M3 live in the exercise files (map-the-access-surface, threat-model-with-stride, author-test-strategy-skill) and in the close prompts of earn-the-trust.md.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early — module N's mood honors arc handoff to module N+1.
rules_evaluated.evidence: M3 mood 'earned trust' resolves WITHIN M3 by design (it's a discipline-installed-before-needed module, not a stew-into-next-module module). Lecture closes on 'Go.' into the three exercises; doesn't reach forward into M4's 'walk what you've built' beat. The team-kit accretion thread (line 26) is introduced but explicitly framed as a horizon ('shipped that way' / 'not a platform-team design document') — leaves the accretion-by-doing for M6 to land.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands across persona-skill variation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The lecture's teaching moment — 'two curated, one authored is a deliberate proportion: frontier moves are curated for you; you build what you know best (your own system)' — is named explicitly at lines 30 ('Why this proportion matters') AND line 49 ('Don't make general what you don't practice yourself'). Two anchors means a fast-skim persona who skips line 30 still hits line 49. Engineer-persona trace confirms recognition at both beats.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity — file honors the module's Big Idea.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Module Big Idea (earn-the-trust.md line 4): 'Before the agent runs bigger work alone, earn your staff engineer's and CISO's trust on a small piece you're shipping this week. Two curated skills from the frontier do the security work, one skill you author through conversation with Claude does the quality work.' Lecture opens (line 6) with: 'Two moves today. You're going to INVOKE two skills this module: one for access-control analysis, one for STRIDE threat modeling. ... Then you're going to AUTHOR one skill yourself: a test-strategy skill tuned to your codebase.' Direct restatement. 'What earn-the-trust means' section (lines 32-40) names the three artifacts staff engineer + CISO would read — direct restatement of the trust-contract Big Idea.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: This file's close hands off to next module's open.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lecture closes on 'Go.' into M3's three exercises (it's an opener inside M3, not a module-closer). The module-level handoff to M4 lives at earn-the-trust.md line 73-81 (Next + Bring to Module 4). Lecture's job is to prime, not to bridge; arc handoff happens at module file scope.
